target.name: Nominal
target.founder: Cameron McCord, Bryce Strauss, Jason Hoch

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + similar-valuation-tier peers in AI & Software. Range: $2.0B - $3.8B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in AI & Compute

data.narrative:
Nominal is a Series B-2 stage AI & Software company currently valued at $1.0B, having raised $155M to date. The valuation assessment draws on a peer universe of 5 same-sector companies at similar valuation tiers, supplemented by reference to a broader comp set including 1 public company (Palantir) and 8 private peers spanning Figure AI, Scale AI, and Applied Intuition.

The peer-median method, the applicable triangulation approach given data availability, identifies a median valuation of $3.2B across the five comparables (Ayar Labs at $3.8B, Together AI at $3.3B, Black Forest Labs at $3.2B, Lambda at $2.5B, and Field AI at $2.0B). This method establishes a reference range of $2.0B to $3.8B, reflecting the spread of realized or estimated valuations in the peer cohort.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $2.8B to $3.7B, centered on a median of $3.2B. This implies meaningful upside from the current $1.0B mark, consistent with Series B-2 stage progression in the AI & Software sector. Key uncertainties include revenue traction relative to peers, burn profile, and the specific technical defensibility of Nominal's approach within a crowded frontier-tech landscape. The single method applied limits confidence; additional revenue multiple or DCF analysis would strengthen conviction in this range.
